text:
Once upon an old oak-tree town, there lived a curious badger named Bucky.

One afternoon, as Bucky scouted through the shadows beneath the tree, he found a tiny, shimmering glass ball. It gleamed like a star, and something within it sang—a melody so sweet, it made Bucky forget his worries.

So off he went, trotting home through a meadow of lavender. Each step echoed the song that followed him, until, finally, he stopped. That glass ball nestled snugly in his burrow.

Its singing stopped.

Bucky poked at it with a paw, listening—it sang no more.

He decided to keep it. Each hour Bucky held the ball, its magic resonated through his bones. He sung a song of his own creation, one that echoed through the forest. It became known as the Badger's Lament.

Friends cheered as Bucky danced through twilight with his newfound talent.
